summary: Migration 010 correctly creates channels, claude_sessions, channel_session_links, sync_cursors tables and renames goal_tasks→tasks with nullable goal_id. Previous audit issue (SessionRepository referencing 'sessions' instead of '_deprecated_sessions') has been fixed (cb8e007). All 3 new repositories (ChannelRepository, ClaudeSessionRepository, SyncCursorRepository) are well-implemented with proper CRUD, upsert, and conversion logic. All 115 tests pass including 40 new tests across the 3 new repos. GoalTaskRepo correctly maps threadId↔channel_id at the DB boundary. Two minor warnings remain but are non-blocking.

issues.description: GoalTaskRow still declares 'thread_id' but the actual tasks table column is 'channel_id' after migration 010. GoalTaskRepo works at runtime because it reads row.channel_id directly (bypassing the GoalTaskRow type), but this type mismatch could cause confusion. Consider updating GoalTaskRow or migrating callers to use TaskRow.

Migration inserts from both sessions and archived_sessions into claude_sessions without ON CONFLICT handling. If the same UUID id exists in both tables, the second INSERT will fail. Consider using INSERT OR IGNORE for the archived_sessions insertion, similar to how channels migration already does.

sync_cursors initial value uses ${Date.now()} in a template literal, meaning the timestamp is captured at module import time rather than migration execution time. This is a minor precision issue — the difference is typically negligible but worth noting.
